Here is the JSON transcript with the relevant portions of the code aligned to the corresponding parts of the transcript:

```json
[
    {"text": "so let's change this problem slightly", "code": ["class Solution:", "    def foreignDictionary(self, words: List[str]) -> str:"]},
    {"text": "so let's change this problem slightly", "code": ["        adj = {char: set() for word in words for char in word}"]},
    {"text": "so then maybe", "code": ["        visited = {}  # {char: bool} False visited, True current path"]},
    {"text": "if we were given an input list of words", "code": ["        res = []"]},
    {"text": "so let's change this problem slightly", "code": ["        def dfs(char):"]},
    {"text": "so let's change this problem slightly", "code": ["            if char in visited:", "                return visited[char]"]},
    {"text": "we're given an input", "code": ["            visited[char] = True"]},
    {"text": "and we know that because there exists a", "code": ["            for neighChar in adj[char]:", "                if dfs(neighChar):", "                    return True"]},
    {"text": "so we're going to be doing a post", "code": ["            visited[char] = False", "            res.append(char)"]},
    {"text": "so let's change this problem slightly", "code": ["        for char in adj:", "            if dfs(char):", "                return \"\""]},
    {"text": "so once we've done the entire dfs", "code": ["        res.reverse()"]},
    {"text": "so once we've done the entire dfs", "code": ["        return \"\".join(res)"]},
    {"text": "is basically that base case that edge", "code": ["        for i in range(len(words) - 1):", "            w1, w2 = words[i], words[i + 1]", "            minLen = min(len(w1), len(w2))", "            if len(w1) > len(w2) and w1[:minLen] == w2[:minLen]:", "                return \"\""]},
    {"text": "but then maybe", "code": ["            for j in range(minLen):", "                if w1[j] != w2[j]:", "                    adj[w1[j]].add(w2[j])", "                    break"]}
]
```
In this aligned format, each block of text is associated with the corresponding code that implements the concepts discussed. If a clip of text did not directly relate to any specific lines of code, it was not included in this output.
